subject_options: Petals Hair and Beauty — a note on local search
How Petals could convert more of its 312 reviews into bookings
One gap worth closing for Petals in Westport
email_body:
I came across Petals through Google Maps while looking at well-reviewed salons in the west of Ireland — 312 reviews at 4.8 stars is a genuinely strong reputation, and one of the better signals I have seen for a salon in the region.

What caught my attention is that Westport draws a significant number of seasonal visitors, but without a blog or content section on the site, Petals is not currently visible for the searches that tourist and occasion-based traffic generates — things like bridal hair or special occasion styling in Mayo.

I work with local businesses in Ireland on exactly this kind of targeted SEO work — a local content plan, service page improvements, and schema markup to help the site reflect the quality of the business behind it.

Would you be open to a short call this week where I can walk you through two or three specific changes that would make the biggest difference for bookings from the site?

Best regards
[Your name]
cta: Would you be open to a short call this week where I can walk you through two or three specific changes that would make the biggest difference for bookings from the site?
follow_up_body:
I sent a short note last week about some local SEO improvements that could help Petals capture more search traffic from visitors to Westport. I wanted to follow up in case it got buried.

The core of what I am proposing is a local content plan and some structural tweaks to help your site do more with the strong reputation you have already built. Would this be something worth a brief conversation, or is there someone else on your team who handles the website?
personalisation_tokens_used: Westport as a tourist destination with seasonal search demand
